rationale: The cited paper appears theoretically central: it develops the licensing effect in sequential consumer choice, with a self-concept mechanism and an external-attribution boundary condition, which closely matches the citing paper’s focus on sequential post-progress choices, disengagement/indulgence, and a relational self-concept process. Given this strong construct/mechanism overlap and a direct DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.43.2.259), this pair is not an obvious incidental mention.
